steps.title: Use 6-hour periods
steps.content: A value that is $129\%$ greater is multiplied by $1+1.29=2.29$. Since the exponent in the choices is $x/6$, let the table input count 6-hour periods. At $x=18$ hours, there have been $3$ periods and the mass is $613.90$ grams.

steps.title: Run exponential regression
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$. Desmos gives $b=2.29$ and $a\approx51.12$. Since $x_1$ counts 6-hour periods, the model in hours is $A=51.12(2.29)^{x/6}$.

steps.title: Confirm the choices
steps.content: Choices A and D use base $1.29$, which means $129\%$ of the old mass, not $129\%$ greater than the old mass. Choice B uses the right base but the wrong starting value. Choice C has both regression values, $51.12$ and $2.29$, so choice C is correct.
